# Logit Link (Bernoulli GLM)

A **Bernoulli GLM** (logistic regression) models binary outcomes (0/1) using a logit link function to connect the linear predictor to the probability of success.

## The model

$$y_i \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_i)$$

$$\text{logit}(p_i) = \alpha + \beta \, x_i$$

The logit link ensures $0 < p_i < 1$ for all values of the linear predictor.

## The logit function

The logit (log-odds) function is:

$$\text{logit}(p) = \log\!\left(\frac{p}{1-p}\right)$$

Its inverse is the **logistic** (sigmoid) function:

$$p = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\alpha + \beta x)}}$$

## Interpreting parameters

- **$\alpha$** (intercept): the log-odds of success when $x = 0$. The probability at $x=0$ is $1/(1 + e^{-\alpha})$.
- **$\beta$** (slope): a one-unit increase in $x$ adds $\beta$ to the log-odds, multiplying the odds by $e^\beta$.

For example, if $\beta = 1.5$, a one-unit increase in $x$ multiplies the odds of success by $e^{1.5} \approx 4.5$.

## BUGS syntax

```
for (i in 1:N) {
  y[i] ~ dbern(p[i])
  logit(p[i]) <- alpha + beta * x[i]
}
```

FANGS evaluates $p_i = \text{invlogit}(\alpha + \beta x_i) = 1/(1 + e^{-(\alpha + \beta x_i)})$ internally.

## Posterior sampling

This model is non-conjugate — the logit link means the posterior for $\alpha$ and $\beta$ does not have a closed form, so FANGS uses **slice sampling**.
